source_policy: All 10 cases are first-party captures (origin_kind=generated-first-party) produced by real, pinned tools (rustc, CPython py_compile/doctest/json.tool, ripgrep, jq, git) over fixed in-repo fixtures under a canonical LC_ALL/LANG/TZ environment. No external datasets are fetched; no network; no hand-authored payloads; license is in-repo.
deviation_justification: Target distribution suggested container/orchestrator and application/CI-log families and a runtime exception/stacktrace. These are omitted with cause: (1) no container/orchestrator runtime is available in the pinned Nix build environment; (2) no deterministic first-party tool emitting an application/CI-log stream is available without a generator (which the scope forbids); (3) CPython runtime tracebacks embed the absolute capture directory in __file__, which is irreproducible across the mandated independent temporary capture directories, so a path-clean py_compile SyntaxError (still a real CPython parse diagnostic) is substituted. The mandatory floor (exactly 10 real public-development cases, >=4 families, >=3 ecosystems) is met with margin (7 families, 3 ecosystems).
interop_findings: Reproducibility finding (pinned RTK 5d32d073): `rtk pipe --filter log`, the semantically-correct reducer for compiler/build logs, is NON-DETERMINISTIC — repeated runs over identical rustc stderr produce different byte output (unordered-map iteration in RTK's log summarizer; observed 5 distinct outputs in 20 runs). It is therefore unfit as reproducible benchmark evidence. The three rust compiler-build cases record explicit RTK passthrough instead; `--filter grep` (deterministic) reduces the ripgrep case. This is a pilot observation about RTK, not a qodec or contract issue.
